decisions.title: AcceptedJournal is the postcommit semantic leaf
decisions.decision: Postcommit recursive semantic aggregation accepts only an authenticated AcceptedJournal. JournalDraft belongs exclusively to the admission lifecycle.
rejected_alternatives: generic journal bytes with an accepted Boolean
one leaf type for both admission and postcommit aggregation

decisions.title: Semantic and proof identity are separate
decisions.decision: Semantic identity commits the ordered accepted journals and their state/effect summary. Proof identity additionally commits programs, releases, profiles, receipts, and tree topology.
rejected_alternatives: recursive image ID is the economic identity
proof-tree root is the semantic epoch root

decisions.title: Segment order is dense and semantic
decisions.decision: Each segment covers a nonempty dense position interval. Parent order is exact left-then-right concatenation; sorting or set union cannot replace ordered composition.
rejected_alternatives: sort leaves by journal hash during aggregation
unordered set of child journal hashes

decisions.title: State continuity is exact
decisions.decision: Composition requires left post-state root/version to equal right pre-state root/version under one exact scope and profile.
rejected_alternatives: host assertion of continuity
same epoch is sufficient
ignore versions when roots match

decisions.title: Descendant uniqueness needs authenticated union
decisions.decision: Transition, nullifier, created-resource, and profile-defined one-shot identity sets are pairwise disjoint across children and exactly unioned. A root alone is insufficient without a bounded witness or proof-carrying accumulator operation.
rejected_alternatives: silently deduplicate child sets
trust parent-supplied set root
assume valid leaves cannot conflict across siblings

decisions.title: Aggregation creates no hidden effects
decisions.decision: Accounting and commit effects are exact checked combinations of accepted child journal commitments. Recursive aggregation itself creates, removes, or reinterprets no ZRM effect.
rejected_alternatives: aggregate-level reward or fee not represented by a ZRM transition
omit child effects after compression

decisions.title: Messages and carry compose at explicit boundaries
decisions.decision: Internal message cancellation requires exact outbox/inbox key and payload matching; carry post/pre roots must match exactly. Unsupported profiles use named empty commitments.
rejected_alternatives: cancel messages by count only
treat carry root as interchangeable with machine-state root

decisions.title: Semantic composition is partial associative and noncommutative
decisions.decision: For the same ordered valid leaves and fixed profile, left- and right-associated composition produce the same complete semantic summary. Reordering is not equivalent and normally makes composition undefined.
rejected_alternatives: commutative merge
tree-shape-specific semantic root

decisions.title: First profile favors explicit bounded witnesses
decisions.decision: The reference composer uses bounded ordered manifests and sorted unique vectors, recomputing roots. Optimized accumulators require a separate profile and refinement evidence.
rejected_alternatives: adopt an unaudited production accumulator immediately
make proof-system-specific accumulators part of ZRM core

decisions.title: DA and finality are not implied by recursion
decisions.decision: A recursive proof preserves only the named DA certificates and semantic relation. It does not manufacture availability, ordering, consensus, or finality.
rejected_alternatives: content root implies retrievability
recursive root implies canonical chain history

non_claims: not approved protocol semantics until independent review and maintainer decision
no canonical byte or hash freeze
no recursive guest implementation or release identity
no data availability, consensus, finality, privacy, or production readiness claim
